stories.title: Cirstea Stuns Sabalenka 2-6, 6-3, 7-5 — French Open Alarm Bells

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: Cirstea stuns Sabalenka 2-6, 6-3, 7-5 in Rome R3. World No. 1 exits with a back/hip injury. First career win over a No. 1 for the 36-year-old Romanian — playing her final season. French Open now in serious doubt. #ItalianOpen #tennis

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: Sabalenka after Rome exit: "Felt like my body was limiting me from performing on the highest level." Lower back. Hip. 14 days to Roland Garros. The women's field just became very hard to call. #Sabalenka #RolandGarros

article.headline: Sabalenka Falls to Cirstea in Rome — Back Injury Puts French Open in Doubt

research.facts:
Cirstea Stuns Sabalenka — Italian Open R3
**Sources consulted:** Al Jazeera, ESPN, Tennis365, Tennisuptodate, Yahoo Sports, Sportbible, Washington Post, Punchng
**Key Facts:**
- Score: Cirstea def. Sabalenka 2-6, 6-3, 7-5 [HIGH — multiple independent sources confirm]
- Round: Third round (R3) — May 9, 2026 [HIGH]
- Sabalenka's seeding/ranking: World No. 1 [HIGH]
- Cirstea's seeding/ranking: No. 26 [MEDIUM — one source says 27th, most say 26th]
- Cirstea age: 36 years old [HIGH — confirmed across sources]
- Final professional season for Cirstea [HIGH — confirmed by multiple sources]
- First career win over a world No. 1 for Cirstea [HIGH — stated as such by Al Jazeera + ESPN]
- Sabalenka called for a medical timeout trailing 3-4 in the final set [MEDIUM — single source (Tennis365)]
- Injury description: lower back connected to the hip, limiting full rotation [HIGH — Sabalenka's direct quote confirmed by Tennis365, Al Jazeera]
- Sabalenka quote A: "I'd say that probably it's like my lower back, connected to the hip, which kind of like limiting me from the full rotation." [HIGH — direct quote]
- Sabalenka quote B: "Felt like my body was limiting me from performing on the highest level." [HIGH — direct quote]
- Second consecutive major tournament upset for Sabalenka (lost to Baptiste in Madrid QF last week) [HIGH — confirmed by story history + current sources]
- Sabalenka's 2026 record before match: 29-1 [MEDIUM — story history says 28-1 after R2 Krejcikova win; one more win in R3 yesterday would be R2 win already. Sabalenka's season record at time of exit: 29-1 per sources]
- Sabalenka has never won Rome [HIGH — confirmed by story history May 8 entry]
- French Open concern: Sabalenka said she hopes to "regain her fitness in time" for Roland Garros [HIGH — Al Jazeera]
**Story History Check:** NEW story — Cirstea upset result. Previous pipeline (May 9) covered R3 as an upcoming match (preview angle). This is the result. Status: NEW STORY.

stories.title: Paolini's Title Defense Ends — Mertens Saves 3 Match Points in 4-6, 7-6(5), 6-3 Win

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text: Mertens saves THREE match points and beats Paolini 4-6, 7-6(5), 6-3 in 2h44m. The defending Italian Open champion is out at home. The crowd at Foro Italico goes quiet. #WTA #ItalianOpen

article.headline: Mertens Saves Three Match Points to End Paolini's Italian Open Title Defense

stories.title: Sinner Def. Ofner 6-3, 6-4 — M1000 Streak Reaches 29, Ties Federer

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text: Sinner def. Ofner 6-3, 6-4 in Rome. 29 consecutive Masters 1000 wins — ties Roger Federer for 3rd longest in series history. Zero break points conceded. Next: Popyrin. The Career Golden Masters hunt is very much alive. #Sinner #ItalianOpen

article.headline: Sinner's Rome Campaign Opens With Dominant Win — M1000 Streak Reaches 29, Tying Federer

research.facts:
Sinner Def. Ofner 6-3, 6-4 — M1000 Streak to 29
**Sources consulted:** ATP Tour official (atptour.com), TennisMajors, SocialNewsXYZ, TennisTonic, Yardbarker
**Key Facts:**
- Score: Sinner def. Ofner 6-3, 6-4 [HIGH — ATP Tour official + multiple sources]
- Match duration: 1 hour 31 minutes [HIGH — multiple sources]
- Round: Second round — May 9, 2026 [HIGH]
- Sinner did not concede a break point in either set [HIGH — ATP Tour official]
- M1000 winning streak: 29 consecutive matches [HIGH — ATP Tour + TennisMajors]
- Streak ties Roger Federer for 3rd longest in series history [HIGH — TennisMajors]
- Sinner's next opponent: Alexei Popyrin [HIGH — ATP Tour; Popyrin def. Mensik 6-3, 2-6, 6-4 in 2h11m]
- Career Golden Masters: Rome is the only M1000 title Sinner has not yet won [HIGH — from story history, multiple prior runs]
- No Italian man has won Rome since Adriano Panatta in 1976 [HIGH — confirmed across multiple prior pipeline runs]
- Sinner's 2026 season record after win: 31-2 [MEDIUM — calculated from 30-2 before match + 1 win]
- First break of set 1: Sinner broke in game 4 (3-1) [HIGH — ATP Tour recap]
- First break of set 2: Sinner broke in game 1 [HIGH — ATP Tour recap]
- 5 unforced errors in first set [MEDIUM — single source]
- Sinner described as using "pure ball-striking and baseline consistency" [MEDIUM — editorial description]

stories.title: Gauff, Andreeva Both Advance — WTA Draw Wide Open

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: WTA Rome round of 16 taking shape. Gauff beats Sierra 5-7, 6-0, 6-4. Andreeva beats Golubic 6-1, 4-6, 6-0. With Sabalenka and Paolini both out, the Italian Open draw is anyone's tournament now. #WTA #tennis

stories.title: Roland Garros 14 Days Away — Injury Chaos Reshapes the Favorites

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: 14 days to Roland Garros. Sabalenka: back/hip injury. Alcaraz: out (wrist). Djokovic: doubtful. Sinner is the overwhelming men's favourite — chasing his first Roland Garros title and a Career Grand Slam. The path is clear. #RolandGarros
